title: run-set.json — the ARM spike run-set manifest
description: The manifest half of a run-set (docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §Evidence integrity; the Rust definition is `arm_harness::evidence::RunSet` and this schema mirrors it field for field). It states what was ATTEMPTED and under what conditions. It deliberately carries NO result totals: a checker that read a total from here would be checking the harness's opinion of itself, which §Evidence integrity #2 forbids. Every number a floor checker may use is derived from `records.jsonl`, whose sha256 this manifest pins. UNTESTED ON SILICON: no run-set matching this schema has ever been produced by hardware.

schema_version.description: `arm_harness::evidence::SCHEMA_VERSION`. A checker refuses a version it does not know rather than silently misreading it.

run_set_id.description: Identifier of this run-set. Golden evidence is immutable; a rerun creates a new run-set rather than overwriting one.

images.description: Every boot artifact, pinned by content and verified immediately before use (§Evidence integrity #3). A run-set that pins nothing has attested nothing.

condition.description: The experimental condition this run-set sweeps (`pinned-solo`, `co-tenant-load`, …).

weights.description: The MEASURED `oracle_model::Weights` used to predict counts. `null` before stage AA-1 has produced them — and a checker handed `null` refuses to check counts rather than substituting a default (task 109: skid margins, densities and count offsets are spike deliverables, never defaults).

skid_margin.description: The N1 skid margin, measured by AA-1. `null` for the same reason as `weights`: a checker cannot bound skid without it and must say so rather than guess. The x86 `skid_margin = 256` is folklore here until AA-1 produces the N1 number.

attempted.description: How many samples were ATTEMPTED. Every one of them must appear in `records.jsonl` with a `sample_id` in `0..attempted`, exactly once — a missing sample is a failure to account, not a pass (§Evidence integrity #6).

records_file.description: The records file, relative to this manifest. Conventionally `records.jsonl`.

records_sha256.description: sha256 of the records file's bytes, so a swapped or truncated one is caught. Lowercase hex; an optional `sha256:` prefix is tolerated.

stage.description: Which stage produced this run-set (`arm_harness::evidence::Stage`). The stage is evidence, not a CLI convenience: the floor checker reads AA-3's exact-landing contract out of it.

exit-reason.description: How a `KVM_RUN` ended (`arm_harness::evidence::ExitReason`). `preempt` is the patched 0004-analogue exit (`KVM_EXIT_PREEMPT`); `signal-kick` is AA-1(c)'s pre-patch host-side kick and AA-3's forbidden fallback.

soc.description: The SoC part, as reported by the platform.

firmware.description: Firmware versions. A sorted map (`BTreeMap`, never `HashMap`): key order reaches the serialized bytes, and the bytes are hashed.

mechanism.description: What mechanism this run CLAIMS to have exercised, and the proof (`arm_harness::evidence::Mechanism`). §Evidence integrity #4: a silent fallback must be structurally unable to masquerade as the mechanism under test — the PR-98 review found an existential-stage harness quietly exercising the stock backend and reporting green.

kvm_patched.description: `true` when the host kernel carries the 0004-analogue patch. Stock KVM (`false`) is a legitimate thing to run (AA-1's pre-patch signal-kick) but may never be reported as if it were the patched path.

host_kernel_sha256.description: sha256 of the running host kernel image. arm64 KVM is built in (`CONFIG_KVM=y`), so the kernel IS the module identity.

expected_exit_reason.description: The exit reason every landing in this run-set must carry. The checker enforces it per record, so a run that silently fell back cannot pass.

patch_marker_observed.description: Whether the patch's marker was OBSERVED in the running kernel (a positive probe), not merely assumed from the build. A run-set claiming `kvm_patched` with this `false` is refused.

image-pin.description: A boot artifact, pinned by content and verified immediately before use (`arm_harness::evidence::ImagePin`). Recording a hash without verifying it is the anti-pattern §Evidence integrity #3 exists to kill.

path.description: Path the artifact was loaded from. Never trusted — the hash is the identity.

sha256.description: sha256 of the bytes actually loaded.

md5.description: md5 cross-reference, per the box-gate image discipline.

verified_before_boot.description: Whether the hash was recomputed and compared IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the artifact was used. `false` fails the checker.

perf-config.description: The perf event configuration behind the work clock (`arm_harness::evidence::PerfConfig`).

raw_event.description: The raw event. `BR_RETIRED` is `0x21` = 33 — retired TAKEN branches (docs/ARM-PORT.md, docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §2). Recorded rather than assumed, so a run that silently counted a different event is visible as such.

pinned.description: Whether the event was opened pinned (never multiplexed). A multiplexed counter scales its count, silently corrupting every measurement.

sample_period.description: The sampling period armed, when the run arms overflow. `null` in counting mode.

pinning.description: Core pinning and frequency posture (`arm_harness::evidence::Pinning`). Pinning is a CORRECTNESS condition on the N1/V1 lineage, not hygiene: the arm64 kernel can miss PMU overflow interrupts on core migration (rr issue #3607), and a missed overflow means `run_until` never breaks out of `KVM_RUN`.

pinned.description: Whether the vCPU thread and its perf context were hard-pinned. The checker demands `true` everywhere except AA-1's one sanctioned migration probe.

core.description: The core they were pinned to. A pinned run that did not record its core is unverifiable.

governor.description: The cpufreq governor. V-time counts are frequency-independent; this matters only for wall-clock numbers, and is recorded so that is checkable.

migration_probe.description: Set ONLY for AA-1's deliberate, bounded migration probe — the one sanctioned unpinned run.

weights.description: The measured per-occurrence `BR_RETIRED` contributions (`oracle_model::Weights`). Deliberately has no `Default` in code and no default here: whether an exception entry, an `ERET`, an `SVC` or a `WFI` is counted by `BR_RETIRED` on Neoverse N1 is exactly the kind of microarchitectural fact this apparatus must not guess.

window_offset.description: The constant offset of the counting window itself. x86's analogue measured n+2; on N1 it is unknown until AA-1 measures it.
